steps.title: Track the median positions
steps.content: The original data set has $14$ weights, so its median is the average of the $7$th and $8$th values. After adding $32$, the new data set has $15$ weights, so its median is the $8$th value.

steps.title: Check whether the median must drop
steps.content: The added value $32$ is below all original weights, so the new $8$th value is the original $7$th value. From the histogram, the original $7$th and $8$th values are in the same interval, so they could be equal. The median therefore is not guaranteed to be less.

steps.title: Check whether the mean must drop
steps.content: Every original weight is at least $70$, so the original mean is greater than $32$. Adding a value below the original mean pulls the mean down. Only statement II must be true. The answer is B.
